Salem had its own orchestra by the 1780s and brass bands played regularly as part of religious services and to announce births and deaths in the community.  Old Salem Museums & Gardens collection of musical instruments and related materials shows the important role played by music in the community.  Other objects in the collection reflect musical craftsmanship elsewhere in the American South.

CuratorialText: The Museum of Early Southern Decorative Arts has led the way in researching and collecting work by Southern artisans, allowing us to better understand the rich cultural complexity of artistic production in the South. While it is true that with only a handful of significant urban centers, fewer painters settled and remained in the South, the painting collection at MESDA ably demonstrates the diversity of training and styles with works by European-trained and native-born artists living in urban and rural settings
lexical_adapted: The Museum of Early Southern Decorative Arts has led the way in studying and collecting work by Southern craftspeople, allowing us to better understand the rich cultural complexity of artistic creation in the South. While it is true that with only a handful of important city centers, fewer painters settled and stayed in the South, the painting collection at MESDA clearly shows the variety of training and styles with works by European-trained and native-born artists living in city and country settings.

CuratorialText: The Museum of Early Southern Decorative Arts at Old Salem Museums & Gardens is home to an extensive collection of prints that document the people and places of the early American south.  These views documented English perceptions of America and served as vehicles to convey a host of attitudes and values and provide tremendous insights into a myriad of social, political, and economic forces that shaped Southern culture.
lexical_adapted: The Museum of Early Southern Decorative Arts at Old Salem Museums & Gardens is home to a large collection of prints that show the people and places of the early American south.  These views showed English ideas of America and served as ways to share a set of attitudes and values and give great understanding into many social, political, and economic forces that shaped Southern culture.

CuratorialText: Pastel portrait of Frances L’Escott. Half-length pastel portrait of young woman with body turned slightly to the right and head slightly to the left. She has auburn hair, brown eyes, with loosely fitted ecru and blue gown. The original wood frame retains its original spruce back with the following inscriptions: “Henrietta Johnston Fecit/ South Carolina Ano 1716.” “I/ Frances Lescott/ Married Peter Villep—.”
lexical_adapted: Pastel portrait of Frances L’Escott. Half-length pastel portrait of young woman with body turned slightly to the right and head slightly to the left. She has auburn hair, brown eyes, with loosely fitted light beige and blue gown. The original wood frame keeps its original spruce back with the following writings: “Henrietta Johnston Fecit/ South Carolina Ano 1716.” “I/ Frances Lescott/ Married Peter Villep—.”

CuratorialText: Portrait of man, bust length, seated in a red upholstered chair, body half-left, eyes and head forward, brown hair, blue-gray eyes, ruddy complexion, wearing a black coat, yellow vest and white stock and tie, brass buttons. The background is a greenish-gray.
lexical_adapted: Portrait of man, bust length, seated in a red upholstered chair, body half-left, eyes and head forward, brown hair, blue-gray eyes, red complexion, wearing a black coat, yellow vest and white stock and tie, brass buttons. The background is a greenish-gray.

CuratorialText: Portrait of man in brown coat and yellow vest with white stock. He has blue-gray eyes and dark hair. He is a half-length figure, facing half left, posed with hand in his vest. He stands in front of a column and what must be a balustraded porch with green trees and a greenish ground in the distance.
lexical_adapted: Portrait of man in brown coat and yellow vest with white neckcloth. He has blue-gray eyes and dark hair. He is a half-length figure, facing half left, posed with hand in his vest. He stands in front of a column and what must be a railing porch with green trees and a greenish ground in the distance.

CuratorialText: Portrait of a woman seated in a Chippendale type chair, half-length, facing forward and dressed with a white high crowned cap, gray dress and white neck piece with a black ribbon and jewel at her throat. She has brown eyes, dark hair. She holds a reddish-brown book in her right hand and is seated at a column base to the right, with red drapery and tassel, and with a balustraded rail and trees in the background to the left.
lexical_adapted: Portrait of a woman sitting in a Chippendale type chair, half-length, facing forward and dressed with a white high crowned cap, gray dress and white neck piece with a black ribbon and jewel at her throat. She has brown eyes, dark hair. She holds a reddish-brown book in her right hand and is sitting at a column base to the right, with red drapery and tassel, and with a balustraded rail and trees in the background to the left.

CuratorialText: Full length portrait of a man in blue uniform with red trim at lapel and cuffs, white vest and breeches with black boots trimmed with gold at the top, white epaulets and neck piece, and black tircorner hat decorated with silver cord. He is posed with sword in right upraised hand and a dagger-like knife in the left, facing half-left, with a tree at either side to frame a fight between American troops and Indians in the background. The soldiers in the back wear round hats, short blue jackets with red facings, belly boxes, and linen overalls. The retreating Indians to the left rear of the painting appear with topknots or scalp locks, and wear battle dress of breechcloths and leggings.
lexical_adapted: Full length portrait of a man in blue uniform with red trim at lapel and cuffs, white vest and breeches with black boots trimmed with gold at the top, white shoulder pads and neck piece, and black three-cornered hat decorated with silver cord. He is posed with sword in right raised hand and a dagger-like knife in the left, facing half-left, with a tree at either side to frame a fight between American troops and Indians in the background. The soldiers in the back wear round hats, short blue jackets with red front parts, belly boxes, and linen overalls. The retreating Indians to the left rear of the painting appear with topknots or scalp locks, and wear battle dress of loincloths and leg coverings.

CuratorialText: Charcoal drawing of the profile of Joseph Lewis, Jr., facing left on pink paper. The drawing shows a gentleman with light hair tied at the back with dark ribbon who is wearing a dark high coat with a white stock. The painting is mounted in the original gold frame with black and gold painted glass.
lexical_adapted: Charcoal drawing of the side view of Joseph Lewis, Jr., facing left on pink paper. The drawing shows a gentleman with light hair tied at the back with dark ribbon who is wearing a dark high coat with a white neckcloth. The painting is mounted in the original gold frame with black and gold painted glass.

CuratorialText: Watercolor on paper of a plantation with an inscription at the base of the painting. The house is a white frame two-story central hall structure with its chimneys on the rear elevation. A fence runs in front of the house with a bridge to the right in the background, indicating an inlet of water. An outbuilding stands behind some trees standing behind the bridge. Several large trees are located in the left foreground, along with some moss, grass and other foliage. A path leads to the house, and two men, one mounted upon a horse, the other standing below him are talking to one another. A body of water is in the background.
lexical_adapted: Watercolor on paper of a plantation with a note at the base of the painting. The house is a white frame two-story central hall structure with its chimneys on the back side. A fence runs in front of the house with a bridge to the right in the background, showing a small area of water. A smaller building stands behind some trees standing behind the bridge. Several large trees are located in the left foreground, along with some moss, grass and other leaves. A path leads to the house, and two men, one sitting on a horse, the other standing below him are talking to one another. A body of water is in the background.

CuratorialText: Charcoal drawing, profile of man, facing right, on faded pink paper. The gentleman wears the uniform of an officer, a dark coat with light facings with buttons at the outside of long buttonholes on the lapel and collar, and epaulets of gold bullion. A black inner collar is over a white stock. The man’s hair is graying and is tied in a knot at the back with a black ribbon.
lexical_adapted: Charcoal drawing, side view of man, facing right, on faded pink paper. The gentleman wears the uniform of an officer, a dark coat with light trim with buttons at the outside of long buttonholes on the lapel and collar, and shoulder pieces of gold thread. A black inner collar is over a white neck cloth. The man’s hair is graying and is tied in a knot at the back with a black ribbon.

CuratorialText: Oil on canvas portrait of a gentleman with dark eyes, dark complexion and a shaven head, who is facing half-right with hands folded resting on a table . His right hand holds a quill pen and the left is holding a document with a name written thereon. He wears a white loose collar shirt with deep red robe and turban of the same red material, and sits in a chair upholstered in a red-brown fabric with brass tacks. The background is a dark green drapery to the left and books on the right.
lexical_adapted: Oil on canvas portrait of a man with dark eyes, dark skin and a shaved head, who is facing half-right with hands folded resting on a table . His right hand holds a feather pen and the left is holding a document with a name written on it. He wears a white loose collar shirt with deep red robe and turban of the same red material, and sits in a chair covered in a red-brown fabric with brass nails. The background is a dark green curtain to the left and books on the right.

CuratorialText: Bust portrait, oil on canvas, of woman wearing a pearl necklace and blue dress with lace trim, in mahogany architectural frame with cut-out corners.
lexical_adapted: Bust portrait, oil on canvas, of woman wearing a pearl necklace and blue dress with lace trim, in mahogany architectural frame with cut-out corners.

CuratorialText: Painting, oil on canvas, of Richard Sprigg of Strawberry Hill, Maryland. Young man facing forward, his body is slightly to the right, his left hand in his vest, wearing a dark beige coat and light silk vest, with white stock. He has a ruddy complexion, dark eyes, brown hair. The background varies from greenish-gray to the lower right to very dark gray in upper left and has lunettes in the corners.
lexical_adapted: Painting, oil on canvas, of Richard Sprigg of Strawberry Hill, Maryland. Young man facing forward, his body is slightly to the right, his left hand in his vest, wearing a dark beige coat and light silk vest, with white stock. He has a reddish complexion, dark eyes, brown hair. The background changes from greenish-gray to the lower right to very dark gray in upper left and has half-moon shapes in the corners.

CuratorialText: Oil on canvas portrait of a woman facing half-right. The subject has dark hair combed upward to expose her left ear, and pearls in her hair that hang down over her right shoulder. She wears a blue silk cross-bodice dress tied with a dark red sash. The pinned-up sleeves of the garment revealing a light under-garment. She is posed with her left arm upon a table, the right at her hip, and foliage painted in the background.
lexical_adapted: Oil on canvas portrait of a woman facing half-right. The subject has dark hair combed upward to show her left ear, and pearls in her hair that hang down over her right shoulder. She wears a blue silk cross-bodice dress tied with a dark red sash. The pinned-up sleeves of the garment showing a light under-garment. She is posed with her left arm upon a table, the right at her hip, and leaves painted in the background.

CuratorialText: A watercolor “en grisaille” of Williamsville, a home in Hanover County, Virginia. The house was built between 1794 and 1803 for William Pollard, II, the second Clerk of Court of Hanover County. It passed to his son George William Pollard in 1840 and remained in the Pollard family until after the death of Harry Todd Pollard, the amateur artist who drew and signed the watercolor.
lexical_adapted: A watercolor “en grisaille” of Williamsville, a home in Hanover County, Virginia. The house was built between 1794 and 1803 for William Pollard, II, the second Clerk of Court of Hanover County. It passed to his son George William Pollard in 1840 and stayed in the Pollard family until after the death of Harry Todd Pollard, the non-professional artist who drew and signed the watercolor.

CuratorialText: Painting, done in oil on mahogany panel, of a young man facing half-right. The gentleman has dark hair and side-burns, and is dressed in a white shirt, tie and vest, and a black coat supporting a high collar. There is an inscription on the back of the panel.
lexical_adapted: Painting, done in oil on mahogany panel, of a young man facing half-right. The man has dark hair and sideburns, and is dressed in a white shirt, tie and vest, and a black coat with a high collar. There is writing on the back of the panel.

CuratorialText: Oil on canvas painting of a man facing half-left. The work is a half-length portrait. The subject has gray hair, dark gray eyes, wears a dark brown coat with a high turned-down collar, a yellow vest, and white stock at the neck.
lexical_adapted: Oil on canvas painting of a man facing half-left. The work is a half-length portrait. The subject has gray hair, dark gray eyes, wears a dark brown coat with a high turned-down collar, a yellow vest, and white cloth at the neck.

CuratorialText: This painting depicts a bend in the Cumberland River near the Hermitage, the home of Andrew Jackson (1767-1845). It was a scene that the artist returned to regularly. Another version of this composition is in the collection of the Greenville County Museum of Art.
lexical_adapted: This painting shows a bend in the Cumberland River near the Hermitage, the home of Andrew Jackson (1767-1845). It was a scene that the artist returned to often. Another version of this composition is in the collection of the Greenville County Museum of Art.

CuratorialText: Painting, oil on canvas, of Charles Town showing the city waterfront as seen from across the Cooper River. A large sailing ship is in the left foreground, the opposite shore in foreground with various figures along the shore.
lexical_adapted: Painting, oil on canvas, of Charles Town showing the city waterfront as seen from across the Cooper River. A large sailing ship is in the left foreground, the opposite shore in foreground with various figures along the shore.

CuratorialText: Painting, oil on canvas, waist-length portrait of a lady wearing a gray satin dress with gray stomacher in front and white lace at neck and sleeves, her hair is pulled tightly back, a loop of pearls in it on the left side of the portrait. Her right hand holds a pink rose.
lexical_adapted: Painting, oil on canvas, waist-length picture of a woman wearing a gray shiny dress with gray front piece and white lace at neck and sleeves, her hair is pulled tightly back, a loop of pearls in it on the left side of the picture. Her right hand holds a pink rose.

CuratorialText: Painting, oil on canvas, of a man facing half-left and seated in a green painted chair. The man has a high foreheard, dark hair, dark blue eyes, ruddy complexion, and is wearing a dark coat mounted with gray buttons, a white stock, and a yellow vest. A red and pearl pin is on his neck cloth. The painting is signed in the lower left corner.
lexical_adapted: Painting, oil on canvas, of a man facing half-left and seated in a green painted chair. The man has a high forehead, dark hair, dark blue eyes, reddish skin, and is wearing a dark coat with gray buttons, a white neckcloth, and a yellow vest. A red and pearl pin is on his neck cloth. The painting is signed in the lower left corner.
